_meta.how_to_regress: npm run check:golden crisis-prioritizer  (dev backend up)
_meta.note: 12-action dispatch; generate max_tokens 5000; cp_ i18n split from ChaosPilot (chp_).

output.grounding_message: You're wired and panicking, and that feeling is real — but let's look at what's actually in front of you. You have one task due today, one due tomorrow, and one with no deadline at all. That's a manageable list, not a crisis.
output.reality_check: Of your 3 tasks, only 1 actually needs to happen today — and even that depends on what it is.
output.actual_crisis_tasks: At most 1 task (due today) is time-sensitive, and 0 tasks have confirmed irreversible consequences visible from the information provided.
output.can_wait: 2 tasks can wait without real consequences — the tomorrow task has a full day of buffer, and the undated task has no deadline pressure at all.
output.estimated_time: Without knowing what the tasks are, a realistic estimate can't be pinned down, but 4 hours is likely more than enough for 1–2 undefined tasks.
todays_actual_must_dos: Task 2 (due today) — it has today as its deadline, so if something irreversible happens at midnight, it needs attention first

anxiety_driven.why_it_feels_urgent: When you're panicking, everything in your field of view feels like it needs to be handled immediately.
anxiety_driven.reality: This task has no deadline — waiting 24–48 hours, or even a week, produces zero concrete negative consequence based on available information.

anxiety_driven.why_it_feels_urgent: Tomorrow feels close when you're already stressed, and proximity to a deadline can trick your brain into treating it as overdue.
anxiety_driven.reality: You have a full day remaining — starting it today is smart, but a full panic-mode sprint right now is not required.

legitimately_urgent.why: It carries a same-day deadline, which means if it requires action to avoid a missed commitment or irreversible outcome, today is the window.

objective_priorities.deadline: Today, July 2, 2026 — this is the only hard deadline in your current list.
objective_priorities.consequence_if_missed: Without knowing the task content, the consequence is unknown — but the deadline is real and should be treated as the first priority.
objective_priorities.anxiety_vs_reality: Anxiety says everything is on fire; reality says this is the one task with a genuine same-day boundary.
objective_priorities.do_this: Identify exactly what 'done' looks like for this task and do the single first physical action — open the file, send the message, make the call.

objective_priorities.deadline: Tomorrow, July 3, 2026 — you have approximately 24 hours of buffer remaining.
objective_priorities.consequence_if_missed: Missing a tomorrow deadline could matter depending on the task — but right now it does not need your full energy, only a plan.
objective_priorities.anxiety_vs_reality: Anxiety says it's basically due now; reality says you have a full day and can touch this after Task 2 is handled.
objective_priorities.do_this: After Task 2 is done, spend 10 minutes clarifying what Task 1 requires so you're not starting it cold tomorrow morning.

objective_priorities.deadline: No hard deadline — this task carries no time pressure based on available information.
objective_priorities.consequence_if_missed: Nothing irreversible happens if this waits — it belongs on a future list, not today's.
objective_priorities.anxiety_vs_reality: Anxiety pulled it into today's pile because you're in a heightened state; reality is that it can live on tomorrow's or this week's list safely.
objective_priorities.do_this: Write it down somewhere you trust and consciously remove it from today's mental load — that act itself is productive.

guilt_free_deferrals: Task 3 does not need to happen today — it has no deadline attached to it, meaning nothing concrete is lost by moving it to later this week or even next week.

Task 1 can wait until tomorrow morning — its deadline is July 3rd, you have more than 20 hours remaining, and doing low-quality work on it right now while panicking is worse than doing focused work on it tomorrow.
output.energy_plan: Spend the first 30 minutes on Task 2 only — wired energy is actually useful for a single focused sprint, so use it on the one real deadline, then take a 10-minute break before deciding whether to plan Task 1 or stop for the day.
